paper.title: RXR-Mediated Remodeling of Transcriptional and Chromatin Landscapes in APP Mouse Brain: Insights from Integrated Single-Cell RNA and ATAC Profiling

glossary.APP/PS1: APP/PS1deltaE9 transgenic Alzheimer's disease mouse model expressing human APPswe and PSEN1dE9 mutations, used to model amyloid pathology
glossary.RXR: Retinoid X Receptor, a nuclear receptor that functions as an obligate heterodimer partner for multiple other nuclear receptors and mediates transcriptional regulation through DNA binding
glossary.Bexarotene: A synthetic RXR agonist (ligand) used to pharmacologically activate RXR signaling; administered at 100 mg/kg/day by oral gavage
glossary.snATAC-seq: Single-nucleus Assay for Transposase-Accessible Chromatin with sequencing, a method to profile chromatin accessibility at single-nucleus resolution
glossary.scRNA-seq: Single-cell RNA sequencing, a method to profile gene expression at single-cell resolution
glossary.ChIP-seq: Chromatin immunoprecipitation followed by sequencing, used here with an anti-RXR antibody to identify genomic sites directly bound by RXR
glossary.TOBIAS: A computational footprinting framework for genome-wide transcription factor binding dynamics analysis from ATAC-seq data, including bias correction, footprint scoring, and differential binding detection
glossary.chromVAR: A computational method for inferring transcription-factor-associated accessibility from single-cell epigenomic data by quantifying motif enrichment
glossary.TF footprinting: Computational identification of transcription factor binding events within accessible chromatin regions based on characteristic protection patterns in ATAC-seq data
glossary.CRE: Cis-regulatory element, a non-coding DNA region that regulates transcription of nearby genes
glossary.peak-to-gene linkage: Correlation analysis connecting chromatin accessibility peaks to expression of nearby genes within a defined genomic window
glossary.LXR: Liver X Receptor (NR1H3), a nuclear receptor that forms a permissive heterodimer with RXR and regulates lipid metabolism genes including Apoe and Abca1
glossary.BINDetect: A module within TOBIAS that estimates bound motif sites and differential motif scores between conditions based on footprint scores
glossary.ENCODE cCRE: ENCODE candidate cis-regulatory elements, a catalog of regulatory DNA regions defined by the ENCODE consortium
glossary.TSS: Transcription start site, the genomic position where transcription of a gene begins
glossary.MACS2: Model-based Analysis of ChIP-Seq, a peak-calling algorithm used for both ChIP-seq and ATAC-seq data
glossary.ArchR: An R package for processing and analyzing single-cell chromatin accessibility data
glossary.Signac: An R package extension of Seurat for analysis of single-cell chromatin data
glossary.permissive heterodimer: An RXR heterodimer that can be activated by ligands binding to either partner receptor, as opposed to non-permissive heterodimers that require ligand binding to the partner
glossary.Apoe: Apolipoprotein E gene, a known RXR/LXR target gene critical for cholesterol efflux and lipid transport in the brain
glossary.Fkbp5: FK506-binding protein 5, associated with intracellular trafficking of steroid hormone receptor complexes
glossary.Hes1: Hairy and enhancer of split-1, a key transcriptional regulator of cell differentiation and proliferation
glossary.Spi1: PU.1 transcription factor, a master regulator of microglia identity
glossary.Nupr1: Nuclear protein 1, a transcription regulator associated with stress resistance and involved in cell-cycle, apoptosis, autophagy, and DNA repair
glossary.Pdgfra: Platelet-derived growth factor receptor alpha, an oligodendrocyte-specific gene
glossary.Nfatc2: Nuclear factor of activated T-cells cytoplasmic 2, an immune regulator that physically interacts with RXR
glossary.out-degree: In a directed network, the number of outgoing edges from a node, representing the number of targets a transcription factor regulates

nodes.claim_text: RXR activation remodels the chromatin and transcriptional landscape of the APP/PS1 mouse brain through cell-type-specific regulatory cascades, orchestrating gene networks controlling neuroinflammation, lipid metabolism, and synaptic signaling.
nodes.supporting_quote: Our study demonstrates that RXR activation remodels the chromatin and transcriptional landscape of the APP/PS1 mouse brain through cell-type-specific regulatory cascades.

nodes.claim_text: RXR activation induces selective rewiring of transcriptional networks while preserving overall regulatory architecture, operating through a three-tiered regulatory cascade: direct RXR binding at accessible chromatin, activation of secondary transcription factors, and propagation through metabolic and inflammatory gene networks.
nodes.supporting_quote: RXR activation induces selective rewiring of transcriptional networks while preserving overall regulatory architecture, demonstrating how nuclear receptor signaling translates into coordinated transcriptional cascades.

nodes.claim_text: Integrating scRNA-seq, snATAC-seq, and ChIP-seq enables comprehensive reconstruction of RXR-mediated transcriptional regulation at cell-type resolution, providing a generalizable framework for mapping ligand-activated transcription factor networks in complex tissues.
nodes.supporting_quote: This study demonstrates that combining scRNA-seq, snATAC-seq, and ChIP-seq enables comprehensive analysis of RXR-mediated transcriptional regulation.

nodes.claim_text: Integration of single-nucleus chromatin accessibility and single-cell transcriptomic profiling establishes a high-quality single-cell atlas of the APP/PS1 mouse brain with all major neuronal and glial populations robustly identified and comparably represented between treatment groups.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq was performed on brain tissue from Bexarotene-treated and Vehicle-treated APP/PS1 mice (n=5 per group), yielding 61,353 nuclei after QC. scRNA-seq from the same model (n=8 mice) yielded 37,640 cells. The two modalities were integrated for cell-type annotation.
nodes.analysis_type: unsupervised clustering and cell type annotation
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: (1) snATAC-seq fragment files from 10 APP/PS1 mouse brain samples (5 Bexarotene-treated, 5 Vehicle-treated), generated using the Chromium Next GEM Single Cell ATAC Kit v2 and sequenced on Illumina NovaSeq S2 PE100. Raw reads were demultiplexed and mapped to the mouse reference genome GRCm38 using the cellranger-atac count pipeline (10x Genomics). (2) scRNA-seq data from 8 APP/PS1 mice (4 Bexarotene, 4 Vehicle), previously generated and published, with raw reads demultiplexed and aligned to GRCm38 using Cell Ranger v7.0.

PREPROCESSING (snATAC-seq): Arrow files were created from fragment files in ArchR (v1.0.1). High-quality nuclei were selected based on two criteria: TSS enrichment score > 4 and number of mapped fragments between 1000 and 100,000. Doublets were identified using the addDoubletScores function and filtered with the filterDoublets function in ArchR.

PREPROCESSING (scRNA-seq): Each library was read into R (v4.2.0) and processed using Seurat (v4.2.1). Cells were filtered by: unique feature counts > 200 and < 5000, total counts < 50,000, and < 10% mitochondrial gene counts. Doublets were identified and removed using DoubletFinder (v2.0). After filtering, 37,634 cells were retained. SCTransform (v2) was used for normalization and scaling.

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) For snATAC-seq, iterative LSI dimensionality reduction and clustering were performed using ArchR default parameters (specific resolution not stated in the paper). (2) For scRNA-seq, principal component analysis was performed followed by clustering using the top 10 PCs at 0.25 resolution. Differential expression was performed using the MAST algorithm to identify cluster marker genes for manual cell type annotation. (3) Cluster identities in snATAC-seq were defined after unconstrained integration with the scRNA-seq profile using ArchR's integration functions. (4) Peaks were called using MACS2 (v2.2.9.1) from within ArchR. (5) Peak-to-gene linkage analysis was performed using the addPeak2GeneLinks function in ArchR to correlate peak accessibility with gene expression. (6) Marker gene analysis confirmed cluster identity by examining known markers (e.g., Aqp4 for astrocytes, Tmem119 for microglia, Slc17a7 for excitatory neurons, Cldn11 for oligodendrocytes, Pf4 for macrophages, Vtn for pericytes/VSMCs, Prlr for choroid plexus/ependymal cells). Chromatin accessibility tracks at marker gene loci were compared to expression patterns.

OUTPUT: UMAP projections of integrated datasets showing major brain cell populations (astrocytes, microglia, neurons, oligodendrocytes, endothelial cells, macrophages, pericytes/VSMCs, choroid plexus epithelial/ependymal cells) with 61,353 nuclei from snATAC-seq and 37,640 cells from scRNA-seq. Proportions of annotated peaks across cell types per sample. Coverage plots of chromatin accessibility at marker gene loci. Peak-to-gene linkage maps.
nodes.supporting_quote: These analyses establish a high-quality single-cell and single-nucleus atlas of the APP/PS1 mouse brain.

nodes.claim_text: RXR activation drives cell-type-specific remodeling of chromatin accessibility, with microglia and endothelial cells showing the strongest changes converging on inflammatory and angiogenic/developmental pathways, respectively.
nodes.supporting_quote: We conclude that RXR activation drives cell-type-specific remodeling of chromatin accessibility, nominates upstream TF regulators with matched motif-expression relationships, and links differential accessibility to gene expression via peak-gene correlations.

nodes.claim_text: Transcription factor footprinting reveals an RXR-centered regulatory hierarchy across brain cell types, with widespread RXR-dependent changes in TF binding and astrocytes showing the strongest cell-specific responses.
nodes.supporting_quote: Footprinting analysis uncovered widespread RXR-dependent changes in TF binding, with astrocytes showing the strongest cell-specific responses.

nodes.claim_text: Neuronal TF regulatory networks remain structurally robust under RXR activation but display localized rewiring, with selective enhancement of specific cascades and shifts in hub influence.
nodes.supporting_quote: Neuronal TF networks remain structurally robust under RXR activation but display localized rewiring, with selective enhancement of cascades (e.g., Atoh1 ->Neurod2) and shifts in hub influence.

nodes.claim_text: RXR ChIP-seq confirms that a large fraction of differentially accessible regions identified by ATAC-seq correspond to direct RXR binding sites, many proximal to gene promoters, and linked to expression changes in neurodegenerative disease pathways.
nodes.supporting_quote: These results demonstrate that integrating ATAC-seq footprinting, RXR ChIP-seq, and scRNA-seq provides direct evidence linking RXR occupancy at CREs to changes in gene expression.

nodes.claim_text: Integrative analysis of ATAC-seq, ChIP-seq, and RNA expression identifies RXR occupancy at cis-regulatory elements linked to key transcription factors, validating RXR's role as both a direct DNA-binding factor and a co-regulatory partner extending its influence into developmental, immune, and stress-response networks.
nodes.supporting_quote: These analyses confirm that RXR acts not only through direct DNA binding but also via co-regulatory partnerships with other TFs, extending its influence into developmental, immune, and stress-response networks.

nodes.claim_text: Motif enrichment analysis correlated with gene activity identifies cell-type-specific transcription factor regulators that shape the chromatin accessibility landscape following RXR activation.
nodes.supporting_quote: To systematically identify transcriptional regulators shaping the chromatin-accessibility landscape after RXR activation, we performed motif enrichment using chromVAR within ArchR.

nodes.claim_text: In microglia, RXR activation bidirectionally remodels chromatin accessibility, increasing access at lipid metabolism and inflammatory response loci while decreasing access at steroid hormone receptor trafficking and homeostatic microglia signature loci.
nodes.supporting_quote: RXR activation increased chromatin accessibility affecting categories such as cell migration (Ccr1), cell differentiation (Dusp6), inflammatory response (Cx3cr1), phosphorylation (Bmp2k, Cbl), HDL remodeling (Apoe, Lipg, Lipc, Lpl)

nodes.claim_text: In endothelial cells, RXR activation bidirectionally remodels chromatin accessibility, increasing access at transcriptional regulation and angiogenesis loci while decreasing access at chromatin remodeling and apoptotic process loci.
nodes.supporting_quote: Endothelial cells showed 1717 differential peaks increasing chromatin accessibility and 928 decreasing it. This cell type demonstrated the strongest overlap between differential accessibility and transcriptional responses.

nodes.claim_text: In astrocytes and oligodendrocytes, RXR activation produces more modest chromatin accessibility changes affecting transcription regulation, brain development, and receptor tyrosine kinase signaling pathways.
nodes.supporting_quote: Overall, gene ontology analysis of genes associated with differentially accessible peaks in astrocytes indicated enrichment of biological categories such as regulation of transcription (Nupr1), chromatin remodeling, brain development

nodes.claim_text: Differential TF binding analysis reveals both shared and cell-type-specific transcription factor responses to RXR activation, with a subset of TFs showing concordant changes in binding occupancy and gene expression.
nodes.supporting_quote: Comparing footprinting changes with differential expression (Figure 4), 20-27% of TFs showed consistent regulation at both levels

nodes.claim_text: RXR-initiated TF regulatory network analysis predicts a cascade from ligand-activated RXR through primary TFs to a large set of secondary TFs, revealing a multilayered transcriptional hierarchy.
nodes.supporting_quote: This analysis predicted a cascade initiated by ligand-activated RXR, involving 25 primary TFs that collectively activate 141 additional TFs, revealing an RXR-centered regulatory hierarchy across brain cell types.

nodes.claim_text: Overlapping ChIP-seq and ATAC-seq peaks are predominantly located at proximal cis-regulatory elements and are enriched in neurodegenerative disease pathways, with confirmed expression changes in associated genes.
nodes.supporting_quote: Many of the predicted target genes clustered in KEGG pathways associated with neurodegenerative disorders, including Alzheimer's disease (AD), Parkinson's disease (PD), Huntington's disease (HD), prion disease (PrD), and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS).

nodes.claim_text: The overall distribution of differential chromatin accessibility peaks across cell types reveals that RXR activation produces the largest effects in microglia and endothelial cells, with progressively fewer changes in astrocytes, oligodendrocytes, and neurons.
nodes.supporting_quote: Differentially accessible peaks were concentrated in microglia and endothelial clusters and less in astrocytes, oligodendrocytes and neurons

nodes.claim_text: Integration of single-nucleus chromatin accessibility and single-cell transcriptomic profiling of APP/PS1 mouse brains identified major brain cell populations including astrocytes, microglia, neurons, oligodendrocytes, endothelial cells, macrophages, pericytes/vascular smooth muscle cells, and choroid plexus epithelial/ependymal cells, with comparable representation between RXR-activated and Vehicle groups.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq was performed on brain tissue from Bexarotene-treated and Vehicle-treated APP/PS1 mice (n=5 per group), yielding 61,353 nuclei. scRNA-seq from the same model yielded 37,640 cells. Both datasets were integrated for cell-type annotation.
nodes.analysis_type: unsupervised clustering and cell type annotation
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: (1) snATAC-seq fragment files from 10 APP/PS1 mouse brain samples (5 Bexarotene-treated, 5 Vehicle-treated; 5 months old, male and female). The first cohort consisted of 5 samples (2 Bexarotene, 3 Vehicle) from cryopreserved cells; a second cohort of 5 additional age-matched mice (3 Bexarotene, 2 Vehicle) was processed fresh. Libraries were prepared using Chromium Next GEM Single Cell ATAC Kit v2 targeting at least 10,000 nuclei per sample and sequenced on Illumina NovaSeq S2 PE100. Raw reads were demultiplexed and mapped to GRCm38 using cellranger-atac count (10x Genomics). (2) scRNA-seq data from 8 APP/PS1 mice, previously published, with raw reads aligned to GRCm38 using Cell Ranger v7.0.

PREPROCESSING (snATAC-seq): Arrow files were created from fragment files in ArchR (v1.0.1). High-quality nuclei were selected: TSS enrichment score > 4 and number of mapped fragments between 1000 and 100,000. Doublets were identified using addDoubletScores and filtered with filterDoublets in ArchR.

PREPROCESSING (scRNA-seq): Libraries were read into R (v4.2.0) and processed with Seurat (v4.2.1). Filtering criteria: unique feature counts > 200 and < 5000, total counts < 50,000, < 10% mitochondrial gene counts. Doublets removed with DoubletFinder (v2.0). After filtering, 37,634 cells retained. Normalization and scaling performed with SCTransform (v2). Additional cell filtering used AddModuleScore with cell-type-specific gene sets to remove subclusters or cells expressing non-selective gene sets.

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) snATAC-seq: Iterative LSI dimensionality reduction and clustering performed in ArchR (specific LSI dimensions and clustering resolution not specified in the paper). (2) scRNA-seq: PCA followed by clustering using top 10 PCs at 0.25 resolution. Cluster marker genes identified using MAST algorithm for manual cell type annotation. (3) snATAC-seq cluster identities defined after unconstrained integration with scRNA-seq profile using ArchR integration functions. (4) Peaks called using MACS2 (v2.2.9.1) within ArchR. (5) Proportions of annotated peaks compared across major cell types and treatment groups (n = 5 per group). (6) Marker gene validation: known markers examined including Aqp4 (astrocytes), Tmem119 (microglia), Slc17a7 (excitatory neurons), Cldn11 (oligodendrocytes), Pf4 (macrophages), Vtn (pericytes/VSMCs), Prlr (choroid plexus/ependymal cells).

OUTPUT: UMAP projections of integrated datasets identifying 8 major cell populations (astrocytes, microglia, neurons, oligodendrocytes, endothelial cells, macrophages, pericytes/VSMCs, choroid plexus epithelial/ependymal cells). Total of 61,353 nuclei from snATAC-seq and 37,640 cells from scRNA-seq. Bar plots of annotated peak proportions across cell types per group. Heatmap of top marker genes by cell type. Coverage plots of chromatin accessibility at representative marker gene loci.
nodes.supporting_quote: Unsupervised clustering and UMAP projection identified major brain cell populations, including astrocytes, microglia, neurons, oligodendrocytes, endothelial cells, macrophages, pericytes/vascular smooth muscle cells, and a cluster containing choroid plexus epithelial and ependymal cells

nodes.claim_text: RXR activation by Bexarotene treatment resulted in approximately 1605 chromatin peaks with increased accessibility and approximately 1310 peaks with decreased accessibility across five major brain cell types, while the majority of peaks (approximately 14170) showed no significant change.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq from Bexarotene-treated and Vehicle-treated APP/PS1 mice (n=5 per group) was integrated with scRNA-seq for peak-to-gene linkage analysis. Feature linkages were restricted to peaks within 100 kb of each TSS, using the union of peaks present in >2% of cells in at least one cell type.
nodes.analysis_type: differential chromatin accessibility analysis
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: snATAC-seq data from 10 APP/PS1 mouse brain samples (5 Bexarotene, 5 Vehicle) processed through ArchR (v1.0.1) with peaks called by MACS2 (v2.2.9.1). scRNA-seq data from 8 APP/PS1 mice processed through Seurat (v4.2.1). Feature linkages (peak-to-gene links) were computed using the addPeak2GeneLinks function in ArchR, restricted to peaks within 100 kb of each TSS, using the union of peaks present in > 2% of cells in at least one cell type.

PREPROCESSING: The processed ArchR project was converted to Signac Seurat Object format using the ArchRtoSignac package (v1.0.5). Peak matrices and cell type annotations were transferred during conversion.

CORE ANALYSIS: Differentially accessible regions between Bexarotene-treated and Vehicle-treated groups were determined using the FindMarkers function in the Signac package (v1.14.0), employing a logistic regression (.) framework. This analysis was performed across five major cell types (astrocytes, microglia, neurons, oligodendrocytes, endothelial cells). Peaks were categorized as upregulated, downregulated, or non-significant based on an adjusted p-value threshold of < 0.05. Peak counts were tallied across all five major cell types.

OUTPUT: A heatmap of differentially accessible peaks (left panel) and their linked genes (right panel) between RXR-activated and Vehicle groups across major cell types, with peaks categorized as downregulated (n = 1310), non-significant (n = 14,170), or upregulated (n = 1685). Z-scores shown for both accessibility and expression levels. Samples grouped by cell type and treatment.
nodes.supporting_quote: Most peaks showed no significant difference between RXR-activated and Vehicle groups across the five major cell types (n = 14,170; denoted "ns" in the heatmap), but 1605 peaks were more accessible with RXR activation and 1310 were enriched in Vehicle

nodes.claim_text: Differentially accessible chromatin peaks following RXR activation were concentrated in microglia and endothelial cell clusters, with fewer differential peaks in astrocytes, oligodendrocytes, and neurons.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq from Bexarotene-treated and Vehicle-treated APP/PS1 mice (n=5 per group) was analyzed for differential accessibility within each cell type.
nodes.analysis_type: cell-type-specific differential accessibility comparison
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: snATAC-seq data from 10 APP/PS1 mouse brain samples (5 Bexarotene, 5 Vehicle) converted to Signac Seurat Object format via ArchRtoSignac (v1.0.5). Cell type annotations derived from unconstrained integration with scRNA-seq in ArchR.

PREPROCESSING: Peaks called by MACS2 (v2.2.9.1) within ArchR. Peak-to-gene linkages restricted to peaks within 100 kb of each TSS, using the union of peaks present in > 2% of cells in at least one cell type.

CORE ANALYSIS: Differentially accessible peaks between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups were identified per cell type using the FindMarkers function in Signac (v1.14.0) with a logistic regression framework. Peaks with adjusted p-value < 0.05 were considered significant. The total number of differential peaks was tallied separately for each of the five major cell types: microglia, endothelial cells, astrocytes, oligodendrocytes, and neurons. The distribution of differential peaks across cell types was compared to determine which cell types showed the greatest chromatin remodeling. A distribution plot showing the effect size (log2FC) for ATAC-seq results across cell types was also generated.

OUTPUT: Per-cell-type counts of differentially accessible peaks (upregulated and downregulated). Supplementary Table S2 listing all differential peaks by cell type. Supplementary Figure S2A showing the distribution of log2FC of differentially accessible peaks across cell types. Ranking of cell types by number of differential peaks.
nodes.evidence_location: Section 3.3; Supplementary Table S2; Supplementary Figure S2A
nodes.supporting_quote: Differentially accessible peaks were concentrated in microglia and endothelial clusters and less in astrocytes, oligodendrocytes and neurons

nodes.claim_text: In microglia, RXR activation increased chromatin accessibility at regions associated with cell migration, cell differentiation, inflammatory response, phosphorylation, and HDL remodeling genes including Apoe, Lipg, Lipc, and Lpl.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq from Bexarotene-treated and Vehicle-treated APP/PS1 mice was analyzed for differential accessibility in microglia, with gene ontology enrichment of associated genes.
nodes.analysis_type: differential accessibility and GO enrichment in microglia
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: snATAC-seq data from microglia cells (subset from 10 APP/PS1 mouse brain samples, 5 Bexarotene, 5 Vehicle), converted to Signac Seurat Object format. scRNA-seq data for peak-to-gene linkage.

PREPROCESSING: Peaks called by MACS2 (v2.2.9.1) within ArchR. Peak-to-gene linkages computed using addPeak2GeneLinks in ArchR, restricted to peaks within 100 kb of each TSS, using the union of peaks present in > 2% of cells in at least one cell type.

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) Differentially accessible peaks in microglia between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups were identified using the FindMarkers function in Signac (v1.14.0) with a logistic regression framework (adjusted p-value < 0.05). (2) Upregulated peaks (those with increased accessibility in Bexarotene vs. Vehicle) were linked to their nearest genes using the peak-to-gene linkage information. (3) Functional annotation of genes associated with upregulated peaks was performed using DAVID (v6.8, https://david.ncifcrf.gov) to identify enriched Gene Ontology (GO) biological process categories.

OUTPUT: List of upregulated differentially accessible peaks in microglia with associated genes. GO enrichment results showing enriched biological process categories including cell migration (e.g., Ccr1), cell differentiation (e.g., Dusp6), inflammatory response (e.g., Cx3cr1), phosphorylation (e.g., Bmp2k, Cbl), and HDL remodeling (e.g., Apoe, Lipg, Lipc, Lpl). Visualized as GO enrichment bar plot (Figure 3C, left panel).
nodes.supporting_quote: RXR activation increased chromatin accessibility affecting categories such as cell migration (Ccr1), cell differentiation (Dusp6), inflammatory response (Cx3cr1), phosphorylation (Bmp2k, Cbl), HDL remodeling (Apoe, Lipg, Lipc, Lpl)

nodes.claim_text: In microglia, RXR activation decreased chromatin accessibility at regions associated with chromatin remodeling transcription (including Nr3c1 and Fkbp5), cell differentiation (including the microglia master regulator Spi1), and phospholipase C-activating G protein-coupled receptor signaling (including P2ry12 and Lrp1).
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq from Bexarotene-treated and Vehicle-treated APP/PS1 mice was analyzed for differential accessibility in microglia, with gene ontology enrichment of associated genes.
nodes.analysis_type: differential accessibility and GO enrichment in microglia
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: snATAC-seq data from microglia cells (subset from 10 APP/PS1 mouse brain samples, 5 Bexarotene, 5 Vehicle), converted to Signac Seurat Object format. scRNA-seq data for peak-to-gene linkage.

PREPROCESSING: Peaks called by MACS2 (v2.2.9.1) within ArchR. Peak-to-gene linkages computed using addPeak2GeneLinks in ArchR, restricted to peaks within 100 kb of each TSS, using the union of peaks present in > 2% of cells in at least one cell type.

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) Differentially accessible peaks in microglia between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups were identified using the FindMarkers function in Signac (v1.14.0) with a logistic regression framework (adjusted p-value < 0.05). (2) Downregulated peaks (those with decreased accessibility in Bexarotene vs. Vehicle) were linked to their nearest genes using peak-to-gene linkage information. (3) Functional annotation of genes associated with downregulated peaks was performed using DAVID (v6.8) to identify enriched GO biological process categories.

OUTPUT: List of downregulated differentially accessible peaks in microglia with associated genes. GO enrichment results showing enriched biological process categories including chromatin remodeling transcription (Nr3c1, Fkbp5), cell differentiation (Notch2, Spi1), and phospholipase C-activating G protein-coupled receptor signaling pathway (P2ry12, Lrp1). Visualized as GO enrichment bar plot (Figure 3C, right panel).
nodes.supporting_quote: Bexarotene also decreased chromatin accessibility in gene ontology categories such as chromatin remodeling transcription (Nr3c1, Fkbp5, both genes are specific for microglia and macrophages), cell differentiation (Notch2, Spi1 gene is microglia master regulator), and the phospholipase C-activating G protein-coupled receptor signaling pathway (P2ry12, Lrp1)

nodes.claim_text: Peaks linked to the Apoe/Apoc1 locus were significantly more accessible in microglia following RXR activation, and Apoe mRNA was significantly increased after Bexarotene treatment.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq and scRNA-seq from Bexarotene-treated and Vehicle-treated APP/PS1 mice were jointly analyzed to link chromatin accessibility changes at the Apoe/Apoc1 locus to gene expression changes in microglia.
nodes.analysis_type: locus-level differential accessibility and expression analysis
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: (1) snATAC-seq data from microglia cells (subset from 10 APP/PS1 mouse brain samples, 5 Bexarotene, 5 Vehicle), converted to Signac Seurat Object format. (2) scRNA-seq data from microglia cells processed with Seurat (v4.2.1).

PREPROCESSING: snATAC-seq peaks called by MACS2 (v2.2.9.1) within ArchR. Peak-to-gene linkages computed using addPeak2GeneLinks in ArchR, restricted to peaks within 100 kb of each TSS. scRNA-seq data normalized with SCTransform (v2).

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) Differential chromatin accessibility at the Apoe/Apoc1 locus in microglia was assessed using the FindMarkers function in Signac (v1.14.0) with a logistic regression framework. Peaks linked to the Apoe and Apoc1 genes were tested for differential accessibility between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups (adjusted p-value < 0.05). (2) Differential gene expression of Apoe mRNA in microglia was assessed using the MAST algorithm within Seurat, with Bonferroni correction applied. A gene was considered differentially expressed if it had a Bonferroni-corrected p-value < 0.05. (3) Coverage plots were generated at the Apoe/Apoc1 locus showing promoter-proximal peaks and their accessibility under each condition.

OUTPUT: Differential accessibility statistics for peaks linked to Apoe (log2FC and adjusted p-value) and Apoc1 (log2FC and adjusted p-value) in microglia. Differential expression statistics for Apoe mRNA (log2FC and Bonferroni-corrected p-value). Locus-level coverage plots with linked peaks and violin plots of RNA expression levels.
nodes.supporting_quote: Peaks linked to Apoe/Apoc locus were significantly upregulated in microglia (Apoe log2FC = 1.66; adj-p = 0.028 and Apoc1 log2FC = 1.13, adj-p = 6.3 x 10-33) with several promoter-proximal peaks showing higher accessibility under RXR activation

nodes.claim_text: Transcription factor footprinting analysis predicted an RXR-centered regulatory cascade involving 25 primary TFs that collectively activate 141 additional TFs across brain cell types.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq from Bexarotene-treated and Vehicle-treated APP/PS1 mice (n=5 per group) was analyzed for TF footprinting across major brain cell types.
nodes.analysis_type: TF footprinting and regulatory network construction
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: snATAC-seq data from major brain cell types (astrocytes, endothelial cells, microglia, oligodendrocytes) from 10 APP/PS1 mouse brain samples (5 Bexarotene, 5 Vehicle). BAM files aligned to GRCm38. Accessible peak regions for each cell type from different samples were called using MACS2 (v2.2.9.1) from within Signac (Signac::CallPeaks) with default parameters.

PREPROCESSING: (1) Tn5 insertion bias correction was performed using the TOBIAS (v0.17.0) ATACorrect module on the ATAC-seq BAM files for each cell type and condition. (2) After bias correction, ScoreBigWig was used to calculate footprint scores based on the accessibility in flanking background regions and depth of the local footprint.

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) The BINDetect module was applied to estimate bound motif sites and differential motif scores between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups based on footprint scores, sequence, and motifs across major cell types. For each TF, the global log2FC distribution was compared against a background distribution obtained by subsampling log2FC values from all motif sites, producing a differential binding score and an empirical p-value. (2) TFs with scores in the top or bottom 5% or with -log10(p-value) above the 95th percentile were considered significantly altered between Bexarotene and Vehicle conditions. (3) To classify individual sites as bound or unbound, each site's footprint score was compared to a background distribution generated from randomly sampled accessible regions; sites meeting a significance threshold of p < 0.001 were labeled as bound. (4) The TF-TF regulatory network was constructed using the TOBIAS CreateNetwork module by linking bound TFBSs within promoter regions to their target genes that encode transcription factors, thereby establishing directed regulatory edges from source TFs to the TFs they regulate. (5) The resulting network was imported into Cytoscape (v3.10.3) for visualization.

OUTPUT: A directed TF regulatory network centered on RXR, with nodes representing TFs and edges representing regulatory relationships (bound TFBS in promoter of target TF gene). The network identified 25 primary TFs directly downstream of RXR that collectively activate 141 additional TFs. Node shapes denote hierarchical levels (circles = direct RXR targets; octagons = indirect TFs downstream of direct targets). Node color indicates mean log2FC in binding score (RXR-activated vs. Vehicle); node size represents TFBS score.
nodes.supporting_quote: This analysis predicted a cascade initiated by ligand-activated RXR, involving 25 primary TFs that collectively activate 141 additional TFs, revealing an RXR-centered regulatory hierarchy across brain cell types.

nodes.claim_text: ChIP-seq with an RXR antibody identified 11957 significant RXR-binding sites in RXR-activated versus Vehicle APP/PS1 mouse brain, of which 3908 overlapped with enriched ATAC-seq peaks.
nodes.experiment_detail: ChIP-seq was performed with an anti-RXR antibody on APP/PS1 mouse brain tissue (n=2 Bexarotene, n=2 Vehicle). After alignment with Bowtie2 and quality filtering, ~35.1M uniquely mapped reads for Bexarotene and ~29.5M for Vehicle were obtained. snATAC-seq peaks were from the same model (n=5 per group).
nodes.analysis_type: peak calling and cross-modal overlap analysis
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: (1) ChIP-seq FASTQ files from APP/PS1 mouse brain tissue: Bexarotene-treated (n = 2) and Vehicle-treated (n = 2) mice at the same age and treatment schedule as single-cell assays. ChIP was performed using rabbit polyclonal anti-RXR antibody (Delta-N 197, #sc-774, Santa Cruz). (2) snATAC-seq enriched peaks from the TOBIAS BINDetect analysis identifying RXR motif-enriched regions following Bexarotene treatment.

PREPROCESSING (ChIP-seq): Reads were aligned to the mouse reference genome GRCm38 using Bowtie2 (v2.5.4) with the local option. Low-quality reads (mapping score < 2) were removed by Samtools (v1.21). After quality filtering, 35.1 million uniquely mapped reads were obtained for the Bexarotene group and 29.5 million for the Vehicle group, averaging approximately 16.1 million reads per sample.

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) Peak calling was performed with MACS2 at a q-value threshold of 0.05, comparing Bexarotene vs. Vehicle groups. (2) To validate genomic sites identified by TOBIAS BINDetect as enriched RXR motifs following Bexarotene treatment, the overlap between ChIP-seq peaks and ATAC-seq enriched peaks was calculated using Bedtools (v2.31.1) with 10 kb genomic windows. (3) Overlapping regions were annotated by Uropa with default options.

OUTPUT: Total number of significant RXR-binding sites from ChIP-seq peak calling. Number of overlapping peaks between ChIP-seq and ATAC-seq enriched regions. Venn diagram or bar chart showing unique and overlapping peaks.
nodes.supporting_quote: Peak calling identified 11,957 significant RXR-binding sites in RXR-activated versus Vehicle samples. Of these, 3908 overlapped with enriched ATAC-seq peaks.

nodes.claim_text: Among the 1251 overlapping ChIP-seq and ATAC-seq peaks, approximately 70% were located at transcription start sites or upstream promoter regions, indicating that the majority represent proximal cis-regulatory elements.
nodes.experiment_detail: Overlapping peaks from RXR ChIP-seq and snATAC-seq in APP/PS1 mouse brain were annotated for genomic position relative to gene features.
nodes.analysis_type: genomic annotation of overlapping peaks
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: The set of 1251 overlapping peaks identified from the intersection of RXR ChIP-seq peaks and snATAC-seq enriched peaks (derived from Bedtools intersection with 10 kb genomic windows as described in L8).

PREPROCESSING: None beyond the intersection already performed.

CORE ANALYSIS: Genomic coordinates of the 1251 overlapping peaks were annotated by Uropa with default options to classify each peak by its position relative to the nearest gene feature (e.g., transcription start site, upstream promoter region, intron, intergenic). The number of peaks at each genomic feature category was tallied. The fraction of peaks at proximal cis-regulatory elements was calculated as the sum of peaks at TSS and upstream promoter regions divided by the total number of overlapping peaks.

OUTPUT: Classification of 1251 overlapping peaks by genomic feature: 777 at transcription start sites (TSSs), 60 within upstream promoter regions, and the remainder at other genomic features. The proportion at proximal CREs calculated as (777 + 60) / 1251, reported as approximately 70%.
nodes.supporting_quote: Among the 1251 overlapping peaks, 777 were located at transcription start sites (TSSs) and another 60 within upstream promoter regions, indicating that nearly 70% of overlapping peaks represent proximal cis-regulatory elements (CREs) likely contributing to gene regulation under RXR activation

nodes.claim_text: Pathway analysis of genes at overlapping ChIP-seq and ATAC-seq peaks revealed enrichment in KEGG pathways associated with neurodegenerative disorders including Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, Huntington's disease, prion disease, and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.
nodes.experiment_detail: Genes near overlapping RXR ChIP-seq and snATAC-seq peaks in APP/PS1 mouse brain were subjected to pathway enrichment analysis.
nodes.analysis_type: pathway enrichment analysis
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: Gene lists derived from the 1251 overlapping peaks between RXR ChIP-seq and snATAC-seq, annotated to nearby genes using Uropa with default options.

PREPROCESSING: Overlapping peak regions were annotated by Uropa with default options to assign peaks to their nearest genes. Gene lists from ChIP-seq and ATAC-seq overlapping regions were compiled.

CORE ANALYSIS: The list of genes associated with overlapping peaks was analyzed in Metascape for multilist comparative analysis, integration of annotation information, and enrichment analysis. Enrichment was performed using multiple databases: GO processes, KEGG pathways, Reactome gene sets, canonical pathways, and CORUM complexes. The enrichment analysis identified significantly enriched pathways and biological processes. The resulting enriched pathway network was visualized using Cytoscape (v3.10.3), with dot size representing the number of enriched genes and sector area representing the proportion of overlapping genes in each category.

OUTPUT: Network of enriched pathways associated with genes at overlapping ATAC-seq and ChIP-seq peaks. KEGG pathway enrichment results identifying neurodegenerative disease pathways (Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, Huntington's disease, prion disease, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis). Circos plot visualizing overlap among gene lists from RXR ChIP-seq binding and ATAC-seq motif enrichment.
nodes.supporting_quote: Many of the predicted target genes clustered in KEGG pathways associated with neurodegenerative disorders, including Alzheimer's disease (AD), Parkinson's disease (PD), Huntington's disease (HD), prion disease (PrD), and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS).

nodes.claim_text: Motif enrichment analysis correlated with gene activity and RNA expression identified 88 high-confidence candidate transcription factor regulators across major brain cell types.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq from APP/PS1 mice (n=10) was analyzed with chromVAR for motif enrichment, correlated with ATAC-inferred gene activity and scRNA-seq expression across major brain cell types.
nodes.analysis_type: motif enrichment and correlation analysis
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: (1) snATAC-seq data from 10 APP/PS1 mouse brain samples (5 Bexarotene, 5 Vehicle) processed through ArchR (v1.0.1) with peak matrix and gene score matrix computed. (2) scRNA-seq data from 8 APP/PS1 mice processed through Seurat (v4.2.1) with normalized expression values.

PREPROCESSING: snATAC-seq data processed through ArchR with iterative LSI dimensionality reduction, clustering, and cell type annotation via unconstrained integration with scRNA-seq. Peak matrix generated from MACS2-called peaks.

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) chromVAR, implemented within the ArchR package, was used to compute TF motif enrichment Z-scores on a per-cell basis across all major cell types. This quantified TF motif enrichment across conditions and cell types. (2) To identify functional TF regulators, Pearson correlation was calculated between the chromVAR motif enrichment Z-score and the ATAC-inferred gene expression (gene scores from ArchR) using the correlateMatrices function, with thresholds of FDR < 0.01 and correlation > 0.5. (3) In addition, Pearson correlation analysis was performed between ATAC-inferred gene expression and gene expression level from the scRNA-seq profile. (4) TFs passing both correlation thresholds (motif-to-gene-score and gene-score-to-RNA-expression) were identified as high-confidence candidate regulators.

OUTPUT: A set of 88 high-confidence candidate TF regulators (listed in Supplementary Table S1). Heatmaps showing chromVAR motif enrichment, inferred gene expression (ATAC gene score), and RNA expression level across major cell types. Side panels showing Pearson correlations between ATAC-inferred TF expression and chromVAR (left) or gene expression (right).
nodes.supporting_quote: We then prioritized regulators by correlating motif enrichment with ATAC-inferred gene activity (gene scores) and with RNA expression, identifying 88 high-confidence candidates (Pearson > 0.5, FDR < 0.01; Table S1, list of candidate TFs).

nodes.claim_text: Cell-type-specific transcription factor regulators were identified, including Pax6 in astrocytes, Lef1 in endothelial cells, Neurod6 in neurons, Runx3 in macrophages, Mafb in microglia, and Nfe2l3 in oligodendrocytes, each showing matched motif enrichment and gene expression patterns.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq motif enrichment and ATAC-inferred gene expression were compared with scRNA-seq expression for candidate TF regulators across major brain cell types in APP/PS1 mice.
nodes.analysis_type: cell-type-specific TF regulator identification
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: The 88 high-confidence candidate TF regulators identified from the chromVAR/correlation analysis (Supplementary Table S1), along with their per-cell chromVAR motif enrichment Z-scores, ATAC-inferred gene expression (gene scores from ArchR), and RNA expression values from scRNA-seq (Seurat).

PREPROCESSING: Cell type annotations were derived from the integrated snATAC-seq/scRNA-seq analysis. UMAP embeddings were computed from both snATAC-seq (ArchR iterative LSI) and scRNA-seq (Seurat, top 10 PCs) datasets.

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) From the 88 high-confidence TFs (Pearson > 0.5, FDR < 0.01 for both motif-to-gene-score and gene-score-to-RNA correlations), cell-type-specific regulators were identified by examining which TFs showed motif enrichment (chromVAR Z-score), ATAC-inferred gene expression (ArchR gene score), and RNA expression (Seurat) patterns restricted to or enriched in specific cell types. (2) UMAP embeddings were generated for each representative TF showing: motif enrichment from ATAC-seq (ArchR), inferred gene expression from ATAC-seq (ArchR), and gene expression from RNA profile (Seurat). (3) Cell-type specificity was assessed by visual inspection of UMAP projections and by comparing enrichment/expression values across annotated cell type clusters.

OUTPUT: UMAP embeddings for representative cell-type-specific TF regulators: Pax6 (astrocytes), Lef1 (endothelial cells), Neurod6 (neurons), Runx3 (macrophages), Mafb (microglia), and Nfe2l3 (oligodendrocytes), each displayed as motif enrichment, inferred gene expression, and RNA expression panels.
nodes.supporting_quote: Examples include cell-type-specific regulators such as Pax6 (astrocytes), Lef1 (endothelium), Neurod6 (neurons), Runx3 (macrophages), Mafb (microglia), and Nfe2l3 (oligodendrocytes)

nodes.claim_text: In endothelial cells, RXR activation increased chromatin accessibility at regions associated with regulation of transcription (including Hes1, Bach2, Id3), angiogenesis, cell migration, and actin cytoskeleton organization.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq from Bexarotene-treated and Vehicle-treated APP/PS1 mice was analyzed for differential accessibility in endothelial cells, with gene ontology enrichment of associated genes.
nodes.analysis_type: differential accessibility and GO enrichment in endothelial cells
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: snATAC-seq data from endothelial cells (subset from 10 APP/PS1 mouse brain samples, 5 Bexarotene, 5 Vehicle), converted to Signac Seurat Object format. scRNA-seq data for peak-to-gene linkage.

PREPROCESSING: Peaks called by MACS2 (v2.2.9.1) within ArchR. Peak-to-gene linkages computed using addPeak2GeneLinks in ArchR, restricted to peaks within 100 kb of each TSS, using the union of peaks present in > 2% of cells in at least one cell type.

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) Differentially accessible peaks in endothelial cells between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups were identified using the FindMarkers function in Signac (v1.14.0) with a logistic regression framework (adjusted p-value < 0.05). (2) Upregulated peaks (those with increased accessibility in Bexarotene vs. Vehicle) were linked to their nearest genes using peak-to-gene linkage information. (3) Functional annotation of genes associated with upregulated peaks was performed using DAVID (v6.8) to identify enriched GO biological process categories.

OUTPUT: List of upregulated differentially accessible peaks in endothelial cells with associated genes. GO enrichment results showing enriched biological process categories including regulation of transcription (Hes1, Bach2, Id3), angiogenesis (Bcam), cell migration (Macf1), actin cytoskeleton organization (S1pr1), and regulation of cell shape (Palmd). Visualized as GO enrichment bar plot (Figure 3D, left panel).
nodes.supporting_quote: Upregulated GO categories were regulation of transcription involved in different cell functions (Hes1, Bach2, Id3)

nodes.claim_text: In endothelial cells, RXR activation decreased chromatin accessibility at regions associated with chromatin remodeling (Sik1, Sik3), apoptotic processes, and regulation of potassium ion transmembrane transport.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq from Bexarotene-treated and Vehicle-treated APP/PS1 mice was analyzed for differential accessibility in endothelial cells, with gene ontology enrichment of associated genes.
nodes.analysis_type: differential accessibility and GO enrichment in endothelial cells
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: snATAC-seq data from endothelial cells (subset from 10 APP/PS1 mouse brain samples, 5 Bexarotene, 5 Vehicle), converted to Signac Seurat Object format. scRNA-seq data for peak-to-gene linkage.

PREPROCESSING: Peaks called by MACS2 (v2.2.9.1) within ArchR. Peak-to-gene linkages computed using addPeak2GeneLinks in ArchR, restricted to peaks within 100 kb of each TSS, using the union of peaks present in > 2% of cells in at least one cell type.

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) Differentially accessible peaks in endothelial cells between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups were identified using the FindMarkers function in Signac (v1.14.0) with a logistic regression framework (adjusted p-value < 0.05). (2) Downregulated peaks (those with decreased accessibility in Bexarotene vs. Vehicle) were linked to their nearest genes using peak-to-gene linkage information. (3) Functional annotation of genes associated with downregulated peaks was performed using DAVID (v6.8) to identify enriched GO biological process categories.

OUTPUT: List of downregulated differentially accessible peaks in endothelial cells with associated genes. GO enrichment results showing enriched biological process categories including chromatin remodeling (Sik1, Sik3), apoptotic process (Ltbp4, Gpx4, Pla2g4a), and regulation of potassium ion transmembrane transport (Edn3). Visualized as GO enrichment bar plot (Figure 3D, right panel).
nodes.supporting_quote: Downregulated in endothelial cells were chromatin remodeling (Sik1 and Sik3, salt inducible kinases 1 and 3), apoptotic process (Ltbp4 is a key regulator of TGFBs that controls TGF-beta activation by maintaining it in a latent state), Gpx4 (glutathione peroxidase), Pla2g4a, and regulation of potassium ion transmembrane transport (Edn3, Endothelin 3)

nodes.claim_text: Neuronal TF regulatory networks reconstructed from footprinting encompassed 134 TFs with comparable network size, density (~0.18-0.20), and global connectivity between RXR-activated and Vehicle conditions, indicating that RXR activation modulates localized TF influence within an otherwise robust architecture.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq from Bexarotene-treated and Vehicle-treated APP/PS1 mice was analyzed for TF footprinting in neurons, and regulatory networks were reconstructed for each condition.
nodes.analysis_type: TF regulatory network reconstruction and comparison
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: snATAC-seq data from neurons (subset from 10 APP/PS1 mouse brain samples, 5 Bexarotene, 5 Vehicle). BAM files aligned to GRCm38. Accessible peak regions for neurons called using MACS2 (v2.2.9.1) from within Signac (Signac::CallPeaks) with default parameters.

PREPROCESSING: (1) Tn5 insertion bias correction performed using TOBIAS (v0.17.0) ATACorrect module on neuronal ATAC-seq BAM files for each condition. (2) Footprint scores calculated using ScoreBigWig based on flanking background accessibility and local footprint depth.

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) TOBIAS BINDetect was applied to identify differentially bound TFs in neurons between Bexarotene and Vehicle conditions. For each TF, the global log2FC distribution was compared against a background distribution obtained by subsampling log2FC values from all motif sites, producing a differential binding score and empirical p-value. TFs with scores in the top or bottom 5% or with -log10(p-value) above the 95th percentile were considered significantly altered. (2) The TOBIAS CreateNetwork module was used to construct directed TF regulatory networks by linking bound TFBSs (p < 0.001 vs. background) within promoter regions to target TF genes, for both Bexarotene and Vehicle conditions separately. (3) Network properties were computed and compared between conditions: total number of TF nodes, number of edges, network density (ratio of actual edges to possible edges), and global connectivity metrics. (4) Networks were imported into Cytoscape (v3.10.3) for visualization and comparison.

OUTPUT: Two directed TF regulatory networks (one per condition) encompassing 134 TFs and thousands of edges. Network density values of approximately 0.18-0.20 in both conditions. Comparison of network size, density, and global connectivity between RXR-activated and Vehicle conditions.
nodes.supporting_quote: The resulting networks encompassed 134 TFs and thousands of edges, with comparable size, density (approximately 0.18-0.20), and global connectivity between conditions. This indicates that RXR activation does not dismantle the neuronal regulatory scaffold but instead modulates localized TF influence within an otherwise robust architecture.

nodes.claim_text: In neuronal networks, RXR activation selectively enhanced the Atoh1-to-Neurod2 regulatory cascade, with Neurod2 emerging as a prominent regulatory hub, while the control network emphasized Foxn1 as a broad cascade initiator and RXRalpha as a key integrator.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq from Bexarotene-treated and Vehicle-treated APP/PS1 mice was analyzed for TF footprinting in neurons, and regulatory networks were compared between conditions.
nodes.analysis_type: differential TF network hub and cascade analysis
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: TOBIAS BINDetect results from neurons identifying differentially bound TFs between Bexarotene and Vehicle conditions. Directed TF regulatory networks constructed by the TOBIAS CreateNetwork module for both conditions.

PREPROCESSING: Networks were constructed as described: bound TFBSs (p < 0.001 vs. background) within promoter regions linked to target TF genes. Networks imported into Cytoscape (v3.10.3).

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) Differentially bound TFs identified by BINDetect (e.g., Meis1, Atoh1, Neurod2 in Bexarotene; Foxn1 and Rxra in Vehicle) were used as seed nodes for network analysis. (2) In the Bexarotene network, the Atoh1-to-Neurod2 cascade was identified based on increased binding scores for both TFs and the directed edge from Atoh1 to Neurod2. Neurod2's role as a regulatory hub was assessed by its out-degree (number of direct TF targets). (3) In the Vehicle network, Foxn1 and RXRalpha were identified as prominent nodes based on their out-degrees and positions as cascade initiators or integrators. (4) Hub TFs were ranked by out-degree in each condition, and condition-specific differences in hub identity and cascade structure were compared.

OUTPUT: Visualization of neuronal TF regulatory networks for both conditions showing differentially bound seed TFs (colored circles), their direct TF targets (gray circles), circle size indicating number of direct TF targets, and color representing mean log2FC in binding score. Identification of condition-specific cascades (Atoh1->Neurod2 in Bexarotene) and hubs (Foxn1, RXRalpha in Vehicle).
nodes.supporting_quote: RXR activation selectively altered specific cascades and hubs. For example, a cascade from Atoh1 to Neurod2 was enhanced, and Neurod2 itself emerged as a prominent regulatory hub. In contrast, the control network emphasized broad cascade initiators such as Foxn1 and key integrators like RXRalpha.

nodes.claim_text: A consistent core of universal neuronal regulators including Klf4, Egr2, Zfx, Foxn1, and Nrf1 emerged with the highest out-degrees across both RXR-activated and Vehicle conditions, forming a stable regulatory subgraph irrespective of treatment.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq from Bexarotene-treated and Vehicle-treated APP/PS1 mice was analyzed for TF footprinting in neurons, and regulatory networks were compared between conditions.
nodes.analysis_type: conserved TF hub identification across conditions
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: Directed TF regulatory networks constructed by TOBIAS CreateNetwork module for neurons in both Bexarotene and Vehicle conditions, each encompassing 134 TFs.

PREPROCESSING: Networks constructed from bound TFBSs (p < 0.001 vs. background) within promoter regions linked to target TF genes. Networks imported into Cytoscape (v3.10.3).

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) For each condition's network, the out-degree (number of outgoing regulatory edges, i.e., number of TF genes directly regulated) was calculated for every TF node. (2) TFs were ranked by out-degree in each condition separately. (3) TFs that appeared among the highest out-degree nodes in both the Bexarotene and Vehicle networks were identified as universal regulators. (4) The subgraph formed by these universal regulators and their outgoing edges was examined for structural consistency across conditions (i.e., whether the same radiating pattern was maintained irrespective of the initiating seed TF).

OUTPUT: Identification of a consistent core of universal neuronal regulators (Klf4, Egr2, Zfx, Foxn1, Nrf1) with the highest out-degrees in both conditions, forming a stable radiating subgraph irrespective of treatment condition.
nodes.supporting_quote: Across both conditions, a consistent core of universal regulators-including Klf4, Egr2, Zfx, Foxn1, and Nrf1-emerged with the highest out-degrees, forming a stable, radiating subgraph irrespective of the initiating seed TF.

nodes.claim_text: Integrative analysis identified seven TFs - Myc, Arid5a, Creb3l2, Egr1, Zic2, Gli2, and Nfatc2 - with overlapping ATAC-seq and RXR ChIP-seq peaks within regulatory signatures, each showing cell-type-specific expression patterns.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq, scRNA-seq, and RXR ChIP-seq from APP/PS1 mice were jointly analyzed to identify TFs with overlapping accessibility and RXR binding peaks within ENCODE regulatory signatures.
nodes.analysis_type: cross-modal TF identification at regulatory elements
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: (1) Overlapping peaks between snATAC-seq enriched regions and RXR ChIP-seq peaks, identified using Bedtools (v2.31.1) with 10 kb genomic windows. (2) ENCODE candidate cis-regulatory element (cCRE) signatures. (3) scRNA-seq data from major brain cell types processed with Seurat (v4.2.1).

PREPROCESSING: Overlapping peak regions were annotated by Uropa with default options to assign peaks to nearest genes. Peaks annotated to genes encoding transcription factors were selected for further analysis.

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) Overlapping ATAC-seq and RXR ChIP-seq peaks were intersected with ENCODE cCRE regulatory signatures to identify peaks within known regulatory elements. (2) Overlapping peak sequences annotated to TF genes were scanned using MEME Suite FIMO (v5.5.7) with default settings to identify candidate RXRalpha heterodimer motifs (specifically PPARgamma::RXRalpha and RXR homo/heterodimer motifs from JASPAR). (3) Seven TFs of interest (Myc, Arid5a, Creb3l2, Egr1, Zic2, Gli2, Nfatc2) were identified based on the presence of overlapping peaks within ENCODE cCRE signatures and RXR motif matches. (4) Cell-type-specific expression of these seven TFs was assessed from scRNA-seq data across astrocytes, oligodendrocytes, endothelial cells, and microglia.

OUTPUT: Table of TF names, JASPAR RXRalpha (PPARgamma::RXRalpha) motifs, coordinates within overlapping ATAC-seq/ChIP-seq peaks, and ENCODE cCRE signatures. Heatmap of cell-type-specific expression of the seven identified TFs.
nodes.supporting_quote: This approach identified seven TFs of particular interest-Myc, Arid5a, Creb3l2, Egr1, Zic2, Gli2, and Nfatc2-each showing cell-type-specific expression and regulatory associations

nodes.claim_text: Approximately 20-27% of transcription factors showed concordant changes in both footprinting occupancy and gene expression following RXR activation across the four glial and endothelial cell types examined.
nodes.experiment_detail: TF footprinting from snATAC-seq was compared with differential gene expression from scRNA-seq across astrocytes, endothelial cells, microglia, and oligodendrocytes in APP/PS1 mice.
nodes.analysis_type: concordance analysis between TF footprinting and gene expression
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: (1) TOBIAS BINDetect results for four cell types (astrocytes, endothelial cells, microglia, oligodendrocytes) identifying TFs with differential binding scores between Bexarotene and Vehicle conditions. (2) Differential gene expression results from MAST algorithm in Seurat for the same four cell types.

PREPROCESSING: TOBIAS footprinting was performed as described: ATACorrect for Tn5 bias correction, ScoreBigWig for footprint scoring, BINDetect for differential binding. TFs with scores in the top or bottom 5% or with -log10(p-value) above the 95th percentile were considered significantly altered. Differential gene expression was performed using MAST with Bonferroni correction (p < 0.05).

CORE ANALYSIS: For each of the four cell types, the set of TFs with significant differential footprinting scores (from BINDetect) was compared with the set of TFs showing significant differential gene expression (from MAST). For each TF present in both sets, the direction of change was compared (i.e., whether increased footprinting score corresponded to increased gene expression, or decreased footprinting to decreased expression). The percentage of TFs showing concordant direction of change (same sign of log2FC in both footprinting and expression) was calculated per cell type.

OUTPUT: Per-cell-type percentages of TFs with concordant footprinting occupancy and gene expression changes, ranging from 20% to 27% across the four cell types. Bar charts showing representative TF occupancy (log2FC) and gene expression changes (log2FC) side by side. Donut plots showing the percentage of concordant TFs per cell type.
nodes.supporting_quote: Comparing footprinting changes with differential expression (Figure 4), 20-27% of TFs showed consistent regulation at both levels

nodes.claim_text: Some TFs including Ets1, Erg, and Gabpa showed stronger binding in the Vehicle group across all four non-neuronal cell types examined, whereas Neurod2 exhibited higher binding in endothelial cells, microglia, and oligodendrocytes following RXR activation.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq from Bexarotene-treated and Vehicle-treated APP/PS1 mice was analyzed for differential TF binding across astrocytes, endothelial cells, microglia, and oligodendrocytes.
nodes.analysis_type: differential TF binding detection across cell types
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: snATAC-seq data from four non-neuronal cell types (astrocytes, endothelial cells, microglia, oligodendrocytes) from 10 APP/PS1 mouse brain samples (5 Bexarotene, 5 Vehicle). BAM files aligned to GRCm38. Accessible peak regions called using MACS2 (v2.2.9.1) from within Signac with default parameters.

PREPROCESSING: TOBIAS (v0.17.0) ATACorrect applied for Tn5 insertion bias correction on each cell type and condition. ScoreBigWig used to calculate footprint scores.

CORE ANALYSIS: TOBIAS BINDetect was applied to each of the four cell types to estimate differential motif binding between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups. For each TF, the global log2FC distribution was compared against a background distribution obtained by subsampling log2FC values from all motif sites, producing a differential binding score and empirical p-value. TFs with scores in the top or bottom 5% or with -log10(p-value) above the 95th percentile were considered significantly altered. Cross-cell-type patterns were then assessed by examining which TFs showed consistent directionality (higher binding in Vehicle or higher binding in Bexarotene) across multiple cell types. TFs showing the same direction of change across all four cell types or across three of four cell types were identified.

OUTPUT: Heatmap of differentially bound TFs between RXR-activated and Vehicle groups across the four cell types (red = higher binding under RXR activation; blue = higher in Vehicle). Identification of TFs with consistent cross-cell-type patterns: Ets1, Erg, and Gabpa showing stronger binding in Vehicle across all four cell types; Neurod2 showing higher binding in Bexarotene in endothelial cells, microglia, and oligodendrocytes.
nodes.supporting_quote: Some TFs-including Ets1, Erg, and Gabpa-showed stronger binding in the Vehicle group across all four cell types, whereas Neurod2 exhibited higher binding in endothelial cells, microglia, and oligodendrocytes.

nodes.claim_text: Astrocytes displayed the most prominent cell type-specific footprinting responses to RXR activation, with TFs identified primarily unique to this cell type.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq from Bexarotene-treated and Vehicle-treated APP/PS1 mice was analyzed for differential TF binding in astrocytes compared to other cell types.
nodes.analysis_type: cell-type-specific differential TF binding comparison
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: TOBIAS BINDetect results for four cell types (astrocytes, endothelial cells, microglia, oligodendrocytes) identifying significantly altered TFs between Bexarotene and Vehicle conditions.

PREPROCESSING: TOBIAS pipeline applied as described: ATACorrect, ScoreBigWig, BINDetect. TFs with scores in the top or bottom 5% or with -log10(p-value) above the 95th percentile were considered significantly altered.

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) For each cell type, the set of significantly altered TFs was compiled. (2) The overlap of significantly altered TFs between all pairs of cell types and across all four cell types was assessed. (3) TFs that were significantly altered only in astrocytes (and not in endothelial cells, microglia, or oligodendrocytes) were classified as astrocyte-specific. (4) The proportion of cell-type-unique vs. shared TFs was compared across all four cell types to determine which cell type displayed the most prominent cell-type-specific responses.

OUTPUT: Identification that astrocytes displayed the most prominent cell-type-specific footprinting responses, with TFs identified as primarily unique to this cell type compared to the other three cell types examined. Heatmap visualization showing the cell-type specificity of differentially bound TFs.
nodes.supporting_quote: By contrast, TFs identified in astrocytes were primarily unique for these cells and displayed the most prominent cell type-specific responses.

nodes.claim_text: Olig2 showed higher footprinting scores in both astrocytes and oligodendrocytes following RXR activation, concordant with higher gene expression in both cell types.
nodes.experiment_detail: TF footprinting from snATAC-seq and differential gene expression from scRNA-seq were compared for Olig2 in astrocytes and oligodendrocytes of APP/PS1 mice.
nodes.analysis_type: concordance analysis for specific TF (Olig2)
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: (1) TOBIAS BINDetect results for Olig2 in astrocytes and oligodendrocytes, providing differential binding scores (log2FC) between Bexarotene and Vehicle conditions. (2) Differential gene expression results for Olig2 from MAST algorithm in Seurat for astrocytes and oligodendrocytes.

PREPROCESSING: TOBIAS footprinting performed as described (ATACorrect, ScoreBigWig, BINDetect). Differential gene expression performed using MAST with Bonferroni correction.

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) Olig2 footprinting binding scores from TOBIAS BINDetect were extracted for astrocytes (log2FC = 0.02) and oligodendrocytes (log2FC = 0.01), both showing higher binding in Bexarotene vs. Vehicle. (2) Olig2 differential gene expression from MAST was extracted for astrocytes (log2FC = 0.09) and oligodendrocytes (log2FC = 0.13), both showing higher expression in Bexarotene vs. Vehicle. (3) Concordance was assessed by confirming that the direction of change was the same (positive log2FC) in both footprinting and expression for both cell types.

OUTPUT: Olig2 footprinting log2FC values and gene expression log2FC values for astrocytes and oligodendrocytes, demonstrating concordant positive changes in both modalities in both cell types.
nodes.supporting_quote: For example, Olig2 had higher footprinting scores in astrocytes and oligodendrocytes (astro-foot log2FC = 0.02, oligo-foot log2FC = 0.01) and also showed higher gene expression (astro-DEG log2FC = 0.09, oligo-DEG log2FC = 0.13).

nodes.claim_text: Representative significantly altered TF footprints included Alx1 in astrocytes, Erg in endothelial cells, Spi1 in microglia, and Neurog1 in oligodendrocytes.

nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: snATAC-seq data from four cell types (astrocytes, endothelial cells, microglia, oligodendrocytes) from 10 APP/PS1 mouse brain samples (5 Bexarotene, 5 Vehicle). BAM files aligned to GRCm38. Accessible peak regions called using MACS2 (v2.2.9.1) from within Signac with default parameters.

PREPROCESSING: TOBIAS (v0.17.0) ATACorrect applied for Tn5 insertion bias correction. ScoreBigWig used to calculate footprint scores.

CORE ANALYSIS: TOBIAS BINDetect was applied to each cell type to identify TFs with significantly different binding scores between Bexarotene and Vehicle conditions. For each TF, the global log2FC distribution was compared against a background distribution, producing a differential binding score and empirical p-value. TFs with FDR < 0.05 were considered significantly altered. Representative examples were selected from each cell type based on significance: Alx1 in astrocytes (Bexa_Veh_change = 0.04, pval = 6.93 x 10^-53), Erg in endothelial cells (Bexa_Veh_change = -0.09, pval = 1.84 x 10^-108), Spi1 in microglia (Bexa_Veh_change = -0.12, pval = 1.61 x 10^-142), Neurog1 in oligodendrocytes (Bexa_Veh_change = 0.05, pval = 9.59 x 10^-47). Corrected footprint signal profiles were plotted for each representative TF showing the Tn5-corrected accessibility signal centered on the motif for both Bexarotene (red) and Vehicle (blue) conditions.

OUTPUT: Footprint profile plots for representative TFs in each cell type showing corrected Tn5 insertion signals around the motif center, with differential binding statistics (change score and p-value).
nodes.supporting_quote: Representative altered footprints included Alx1 in astrocytes, Erg in endothelial cells, Spi1 in microglia, and Neurog1 in oligodendrocytes

nodes.claim_text: Chromatin accessibility patterns at cell type-specific marker gene loci paralleled their expression patterns across major brain cell populations.

nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: (1) snATAC-seq data from 10 APP/PS1 mouse brain samples processed through ArchR (v1.0.1) with cell type annotations from unconstrained integration with scRNA-seq. (2) scRNA-seq data from 8 APP/PS1 mice processed through Seurat (v4.2.1) with cell type annotations.

PREPROCESSING: snATAC-seq peaks called by MACS2 (v2.2.9.1) within ArchR. scRNA-seq data normalized with SCTransform (v2). Cell types annotated using marker genes identified by MAST algorithm.

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) Coverage plots of chromatin accessibility at cell type-specific marker gene loci were generated from snATAC-seq data across all major cell types. Marker genes examined included Aqp4 (astrocytes), Tmem119 (microglia), Slc17a7 (excitatory neurons), Cldn11 (oligodendrocytes), Pf4 (macrophages), Vtn (pericytes/VSMCs), and Prlr (choroid plexus/ependymal cells). (2) Expression patterns of the same marker genes were examined from scRNA-seq data. (3) Chromatin accessibility patterns were visually compared with expression patterns to assess concordance. (4) Peak-to-gene linkage analysis was performed using the addPeak2GeneLinks function in ArchR to identify correlated regions of chromatin accessibility and gene activity across distinct cell populations. This function computes correlations between peak accessibility and gene expression for peaks within a defined genomic window of each gene (the paper used peaks within 100 kb of TSS, with the union of peaks present in > 2% of cells in at least one cell type).

OUTPUT: Coverage plots showing chromatin accessibility across major cell types for representative marker genes. Peak-to-gene linkage maps showing correlated regions of chromatin accessibility and gene activity across cell populations.

nodes.claim_text: RXR/Nfatc2 co-binding was significantly altered by RXR activation, supporting Nfatc2 as responsive to retinoid signaling in the brain.
nodes.experiment_detail: RXR ChIP-seq and snATAC-seq from Bexarotene-treated and Vehicle-treated APP/PS1 mice were jointly analyzed to assess RXR/Nfatc2 co-binding changes.
nodes.analysis_type: differential co-binding analysis at Nfatc2 locus
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: (1) Overlapping peaks between snATAC-seq enriched regions and RXR ChIP-seq peaks at or near the Nfatc2 locus, identified using Bedtools (v2.31.1) with 10 kb genomic windows. (2) TOBIAS BINDetect differential binding results for RXR and Nfatc2 motifs.

PREPROCESSING: ChIP-seq reads aligned to GRCm38 using Bowtie2 (v2.5.4) with local option; low-quality reads (mapping score < 2) removed by Samtools (v1.21). Peak calling with MACS2 at q-value threshold of 0.05. snATAC-seq peaks called by MACS2 within ArchR. Overlap calculated using Bedtools with 10 kb genomic windows. Overlapping regions annotated by Uropa with default options.

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) Overlapping ATAC-seq and ChIP-seq peaks at the Nfatc2 locus were identified from the full set of overlapping peaks (Supplementary Table S4). (2) RXR/Nfatc2 co-binding was assessed by examining whether both RXR ChIP-seq binding and ATAC-seq motif enrichment for Nfatc2 were present at the same genomic regions. (3) The significance of altered co-binding following RXR activation was determined from the differential binding analysis (TOBIAS BINDetect results and/or differential ChIP-seq peak calling). (4) Overlapping peak sequences annotated to the Nfatc2 gene were scanned using MEME Suite FIMO (v5.5.7) with default settings to identify candidate RXRalpha heterodimer motifs.

OUTPUT: Identification of RXR/Nfatc2 co-binding sites at overlapping ATAC-seq and ChIP-seq peaks. Evidence of significantly altered co-binding following RXR activation. Data listed in Supplementary Table S4.

nodes.claim_text: Gene ontology analysis of genes associated with differentially accessible peaks in astrocytes indicated enrichment of biological categories including regulation of transcription, chromatin remodeling, and brain development.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq from Bexarotene-treated and Vehicle-treated APP/PS1 mice was analyzed for differential accessibility in astrocytes, with gene ontology enrichment of associated genes.
nodes.analysis_type: GO enrichment analysis of differentially accessible peaks in astrocytes
nodes.analysis_detail:
INPUT DATA: snATAC-seq data from astrocytes (subset from 10 APP/PS1 mouse brain samples, 5 Bexarotene, 5 Vehicle), converted to Signac Seurat Object format. scRNA-seq data for peak-to-gene linkage.

PREPROCESSING: Peaks called by MACS2 (v2.2.9.1) within ArchR. Peak-to-gene linkages computed using addPeak2GeneLinks in ArchR, restricted to peaks within 100 kb of each TSS, using the union of peaks present in > 2% of cells in at least one cell type.

CORE ANALYSIS: (1) Differentially accessible peaks in astrocytes between Bexarotene and Vehicle conditions were identified using the FindMarkers function in Signac (v1.14.0) with a logistic regression framework (adjusted p-value < 0.05). Total of 276 differential peaks (26 upregulated, 252 downregulated). (2) Differentially accessible peaks (both up and down) were linked to their nearest genes using peak-to-gene linkage information. (3) Functional annotation of genes associated with all differentially accessible peaks was performed using DAVID (v6.8) to identify enriched GO biological process categories.

OUTPUT: GO enrichment results showing enriched biological process categories including regulation of transcription (Nupr1), chromatin remodeling, and brain development. Visualized in Supplementary Figure S2B.
nodes.supporting_quote: Overall, gene ontology analysis of genes associated with differentially accessible peaks in astrocytes indicated enrichment of biological categories such as regulation of transcription (Nupr1), chromatin remodeling, brain development
